channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: What is next for Florida's insurance market as CFO Patronis steps down
item.description: He is the state's top insurance official, but Florida's Chief Financial Officer Jimmy Patronis is stepping down in a few months to run for Congress. Governor Ron DeSantis will appoint a temporary replacement to the CFO position, as Florida residents continue to face a fragile insurance market.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Senate eyes late-night vote on government funding after House approves bill to prevent shutdown
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Hours clicking toward a midnight government shutdown deadline, the Senate was preparing to give final passage late Friday to a new plan that would temporarily fund federal operations and disaster aid, but drops President-elect Donald Trump's demands for a debt limit increase into t
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, FARNOUSH AMIRI and MATT BROWN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Adams says Dems 'missed the memo' on migrant crisis and it hurt the party
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Mayor Eric Adams sat down with PIX on Politics host Dan Mannarino for an exclusive year-end interview, looking back at what was undoubtedly a very eventful 2024 and ahead to what’s in store for 2025.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Government funding difficulties create gloom for federal workers before Christmas
item.description: Johnny Zuagar says he’s tried to hide his worries about a potential government shutdown from his three boys as he weighs how much to spend on Christmas presents.
item.dc:creator: Brian Witte, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: US says it killed ISIS leader in Syria in 'targeted' airstrike
item.description: The U.S. military killed ISIS leader Abu Yusif and another member in an airstrike conducted in Syria on Thursday, U.S. Central Command said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Musk helped kill a congressional spending bill. But much of what he spread was misinformation
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump's billionaire ally Elon Musk played a key role this week in killing a bipartisan funding proposal that would have prevented a government shutdown, railing against the plan in a torrent of more than 100 X posts that included multiple false claims.
item.dc:creator: MELISSA GOLDIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: PA lawmakers demand Scranton revert 'Biden Expy' name, calling it a 'scar' following Kids-4-Cash pardon
item.description: Several lawmakers are calling on Scranton to un-rename the President Biden Expressway, citing the damage done to children by now-pardoned Judge Michael Conahan.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden and the first lady bring holiday cheer to patients and families at a children's hospital
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden and his wife, Jill, brought some Christmastime cheer to patients and their families at a children's hospital on Friday but a toddler in a light blue jumper entertained, too.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White House pressed on Biden refusing to speak publicly ahead of shutdown
item.description: The White House refused to discuss President Biden’s lack of public appearances amid the ongoing spending bill crisis as a government shutdown looms.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden canceling student loan debt for 55K public service workers
item.description: President Biden announced on Friday he would be canceling more student debt for tens of thousands of public service workers, possibly his last major move on the issue before leaving office in a month.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Rising threats are driving local officials out of office, new California study shows
item.description: There has been a rise in threats made against local elected officials and candidates, prompting some to consider leaving their post or change their stance on a specific issue due to a 'climate of fear,' according to a recent study out of the University of San Diego.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, Fox 5

item.title: University of California campuses resolve discrimination complaints stemming from Gaza protests
item.description: The U.S. Department of Education said Friday it has reached an agreement with the University of California system resolving complaints from Jewish and Muslim students of discrimination and harassment during protests last spring over the war in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Jocelyn Gecker, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Biden's decline widely reported in final days of presidency
item.description: White House Press Secretary Karine-Jean Pierre is set to face questions regarding a scathing report on Biden's health during his presidency.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Top Senate Dems pour cold water on latest GOP spending bill plans: 'Ready to stay' through Christmas
item.description: Top Democrats urged Republicans to return to the original stopgap spending bill in order to avoid a partial government shutdown at midnight.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Watchdog seeks to halt 11th-hour Biden DOJ effort to ‘handcuff’ Kentucky police over Breonna Taylor incident
item.description: The Oversight Project is filing an amicus brief with a western Kentucky federal court seeking to warn against the approval of a consent decree it says will hamstring police.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Timing up in the air for Congress to vote on spending bill as government shutdown looms
item.description: Republican Rep. Anna Paulina Luna told reporters that a vote on the CR is expected Friday morning, but that "there will be no deals with the Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Senate eyes late-night vote on government funding after House approves bill to prevent shutdown
item.description: Hours clicking toward a midnight government shutdown deadline, the Senate was preparing to give final passage late Friday to a new plan that would temporarily fund federal operations and disaster aid, but drops President-elect Donald Trump's demands for a debt limit increase into the new year.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump was poised to inherit a strong economy. Then things got rocky and he added to the uncertainty
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — The smooth economy that Donald Trump was poised to inherit suddenly looks a bit rockier — with critics saying the president-elect is contributing to the uncertainty. The Dow Jones stock index essentially ended Thursday flat after having posted 10 days of losses.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump’s reelection victory pushes some LGTBQ Minneapolis residents to safety training
item.description: For some marginalized communities the second Trump term is fraught with fears over personal safety. In Minneapolis, one organization is helping with de-escalation services and gun training.
item.dc:creator: Odette Yousef, MPR News

item.title: White House starts scrapping pending regulations on transgender athletes and student debt
item.description: President Joe Biden’s administration has started to withdraw pending regulations that won’t be finalized by the time he leaves office.
item.dc:creator: Collin Binkley, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: House rejects Trump-backed plan on government shutdown, leaving next steps uncertain
item.description: With a resounding no, the House rejected President-elect Donald Trump’s new plan Thursday to fund federal operations and suspend the debt ceiling a day before a government shutdown, as Democrats and dozens of Republicans refused to accommodate his sudden demands.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: FAA bans drones in parts of New Jersey
item.description: The FAA warned that "deadly force" could be used against the drones if they pose an "imminent security threat.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Advocates seek to prepare for possible ‘mass deportations’ without fearmongering
item.description: Minnesota immigrant rights groups say they want to avoid evoking fear in their communities, but are finding ways to stay prepared ahead of the next Trump presidency.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Thamer, MPR News

item.title: Trump border czar: 100K beds, more ICE agents needed for deportations
item.description: “This will be an expense — this will be an expensive operation. But in the long run, it should be a — it would be a huge tax savings on the American people,” Homan said.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Johnson spending deal throws Speakership into question as floor vote approaches
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson’s handling of an end-of-year spending deal is throwing his grasp on the gavel into uncertain territory ahead of a critical Jan. 3 Speaker vote, as some GOP lawmakers question their support for the Louisiana Republican. Rep. Thomas Massie (R-Ky.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Vance meets with Johnson after Trump torpedoes spending bill
item.description: Vice President-elect JD Vance met with Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) on Wednesday night, hours after President-elect Trump torpedoed the GOP leader’s spending deal.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Jeffries suggests Democrats will oppose a 'clean' funding bill after Johnson's plan falters
item.description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries (D-N.Y.) suggested Wednesday that Democrats will oppose any federal spending bill that strays from the bipartisan deal announced a day prior, accusing GOP leaders of reneging on the agreement and risking a government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: Fani Willis disqualified as prosecutor on Trump Georgia election case
item.description: A Georgia appeals court booted Fulton County District Attorney Fani Willis's (D) office from the 2020 election interference case against President-elect Trump on Thursday due to her relationship with a top prosecutor on the case.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: After losing VP bid, Walz searches for role in pushing back against Trump
item.description: Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz saw his national profile skyrocket when he became Vice President Harris’ running mate. Now, he’s back home figuring out when he will push back or work with Republicans.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Former top aide to Mayor Adams expected in court on criminal charges: sources
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A former top aide to New York City Mayor Eric Adams is expected in court Thursday to face corruption charges in the latest blow to an administration beset by searches, resignations and the mayor's own indictment.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: House Ethics Committee secretly voted to release Matt Gaetz ethics report, source says
item.description: The House Ethics Committee has voted in secret to release the long-awaited ethics report into former Rep. Matt Gaetz.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Paxton files motion to block auction of border wall materials
item.description: Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton filed a motion Tuesday to stop the sale of pieces of the border wall.
item.dc:creator: Abigail Jones, NewsNation

item.title: Rep. Angie Craig chosen as top Democrat on U.S. House Agriculture Committee
item.description: Minnesota 2nd District Rep. Angie Craig was elected Tuesday to serve as the top Democrat on the U.S. House Agriculture Committee.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: Trump tariff plan threatens to drive up gas prices
item.description: Trump said that on his first day in office, he would place 25 percent tariffs on all Canadian and Mexican goods. The two countries are the leading sources of U.S. oil imports.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: Former FBI informant pleads guilty to fabricating Biden claims
item.description: A former FBI informant on Monday entered a guilty plea on four federal charges related to falsifying statements that in part spurred a GOP congressional investigation into the Biden family.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: TikTok asks the Supreme Court for an emergency order to block a U.S. ban unless it’s sold
item.description: A similar plea was filed by content creators who rely on the platform for income and some of TikTok’s more than 170 million users in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: NYC Mayor Adams denied public funds for 2025 reelection campaign: CFB
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Mayor Eric Adams was denied public funds for his 2025 reelection campaign, the New York City Campaign Finance Board announced on Monday. The CFB cited the federal charges against Adams as one of the reasons why it denied him the money.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11
